articles.headline: How Low Can Wheat Prices Go?
articles.summary: Wheat prices remain under pressure in late August 2025 as the 2025 crop year progresses past the harvest season, as the market considers the July CBOT contract the new crop contract month. Nearby wheat prices continue to slip, with the September contract trading around the $5 per bushel level.

articles.headline: Boss Energy (ASX:BOE) Price Surges 15% Over One Week
articles.summary: Boss Energy (ASX:BOE) experienced a price increase of 15% over the past week, possibly responding to significant events worldwide. articles.headline: Bridgewater Shies Away From China: Time for Inverse China ETFs?
articles.summary: Bridgewater exited positions in Chinese equities, as U.S.-China tensions are concerning for Chinese shares. If you are also a believer of this theory, you can bet on inverse ETFs like YXI, FXP and YANG.

articles.headline: Dollar falls as Trump calls on Fed's Cook to resign
articles.summary: NEW YORK (Reuters) -The dollar fell on Wednesday after U.S. President Donald Trump called on Federal Reserve Governor Lisa Cook to resign, but pared gains after minutes from the Fed's latest meeting showed that only two policymakers supported an interest rate cut last month. articles.headline: Taiwan's Compal Electronics boosts US operations with $300 million investment
articles.summary: TAIPEI (Reuters) -Taiwan’s Compal Electronics said on Tuesday its board has approved a $300 million investment plan in the United States to strengthen its operations and competitiveness in North America, with a focus on expanding its server business.  The plan includes a $225 million cash injection into its wholly owned U.S. unit, Compal Americas and $75 million to set up new subsidiaries that wil

articles.headline: Weak Indian rupee may blunt US tariff bite, say economists
articles.summary: MUMBAI (Reuters) -The Indian rupee's recent slide may help soften the blow from higher U.S. tariffs, economists said, with trade tensions between New Delhi and Washington intensifying under President Donald Trump.  Bankers said the currency would probably have breached the 88 mark if not for likely intervention by the Reserve Bank of India via state-run banks.  The steeper U.S. tariffs imposed on

articles.headline: US defense bill proposes examination of Apple display supplier
articles.summary: SAN FRANCISCO (Reuters) -A measure added into a massive U.S. defense spending bill in recent weeks will, if passed, ask the Pentagon to determine whether one of Apple's display suppliers should be listed as a Chinese military company.  Being on the list does not block companies from doing business in the U.S. but will in coming years block them from being part of the U.S. military's supply chain.

articles.headline: Market Minute 8-1-25- Stocks Slide on Tariff, Job Woes
articles.summary: Stocks are sliding around the world as tariffs strike with full force – and as the latest jobs figures left a lot to be desired. Treasuries are rallying, the dollar is dropping, and gold and silver are climbing.

articles.headline: China's solar giants quietly shed a third of their workforces last year
articles.summary: BEIJING, August 1 (Reuters) -China's biggest solar firms shed nearly one-third of their workforces last year, company filings show, as one of the industries hand-picked by Beijing to drive economic growth grapples with falling prices and steep losses.
